cases.name: star glob pattern: star matches all files
star glob pattern: star with prefix
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/file1 /tmp/gt/file2 /tmp/gt/other; cd /tmp/gt; echo file*; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: star glob pattern: star with suffix
star glob pattern: star no match returns literal
star glob pattern: star in for loop
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/a /tmp/gt/b; cd /tmp/gt; for f in *; do echo "$f"; done; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: question mark pattern: question mark single char
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/a1 /tmp/gt/a2 /tmp/gt/a12; cd /tmp/gt; echo a?; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: question mark pattern: question mark in middle
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/cat /tmp/gt/cot /tmp/gt/cut; cd /tmp/gt; echo c?t; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: question mark pattern: multiple question marks
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/ab /tmp/gt/abc /tmp/gt/abcd; cd /tmp/gt; echo a??; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: glob quoting: quoted glob is literal
glob quoting: single quoted glob is literal
glob quoting: escaped star is literal
glob with paths: absolute path glob
glob with paths: relative path glob
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt/sub; touch /tmp/gt/sub/a; cd /tmp/gt; echo sub/*; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: glob expansion in commands: glob in ls
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/a.txt /tmp/gt/b.txt; ls /tmp/gt/*.txt | wc -l | tr -d ' '; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: glob expansion in commands: glob in cat
glob expansion in commands: glob count with wc
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt; touch /tmp/gt/a /tmp/gt/b /tmp/gt/c; cd /tmp/gt; echo * | wc -w | tr -d ' '; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: glob edge cases: empty directory
glob edge cases: dot files not matched by star
glob edge cases: multiple patterns
directory wildcards: star in directory segment
directory wildcards: star in directory segment matches multiple
directory wildcards: star in directory segment with file pattern
directory wildcards: question mark in directory segment
directory wildcards: directory wildcard no match returns literal
directory wildcards: multiple directory wildcards
directory wildcards: trailing slash preserved in directory glob
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt/a /tmp/gt/b; for d in /tmp/gt/*/; do echo "$d"; done; rm -rf /tmp/gt

cases.name: directory wildcards: trailing slash used in path construction
cases.script: mkdir -p /tmp/gt/sub; touch /tmp/gt/sub/file.txt; for d in /tmp/gt/*/; do echo "${d}file.txt"; done; rm -rf /tmp/gt
